architecture.summary: A survey categorizing how large language models augment reinforcement learning agents through four functional roles: information processor (interpreting observations, language feedback, task descriptions), reward designer (specifying or learning reward functions), decision-maker (policy generation, planning, action selection), and generator (environment/task/data synthesis). Information flows from the RL agent-environment loop through the LLM to enhance sample efficiency, multitask learning, and high-level task planning.

biblio.title: Survey on Large Language Model-Enhanced Reinforcement Learning: Concept, Taxonomy, and Methods
biblio.venue: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems

claims.statement: Large language models with extensive pretrained knowledge and high-level general capabilities emerge as a promising avenue to augment reinforcement learning in aspects such as multitask learning, sample efficiency, and high-level task planning.
LLMs function in four roles in RL: information processor, reward designer, decision-maker, and generator.
As an information processor, LLMs interpret observations, language feedback, and task descriptions to enhance state representation and understanding in RL.
As a reward designer, LLMs specify or learn reward functions to guide RL agent behavior.
As a decision-maker, LLMs generate policies, perform planning, and select actions for RL agents.
As a generator, LLMs synthesize environments, tasks, and training data to support RL learning.
LLM-enhanced RL has potential applications in robotics, autonomous driving, and energy systems.
